entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop (Sorghum bicolor) produced globally, especially in India, used for food and fodder

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: A continent contributing 20% of global sorghum production

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The largest producer of sorghum in Asia

entities.name: RAINY-SEASON
entities.description: A growing season from June to October accounting for 60% of sorghum production in India

entities.name: POSTRAINY SEASON
entities.description: A growing season from September to January for sorghum in India

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Regions where resource-poor, small-scale producers grow sorghum

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food, for which sorghum is an important crop

entities.name: STOVER
entities.description: The leaves and stalks of sorghum left after grain harvest, used as dry fodder

entities.name: DRY FODDER
entities.description: Animal feed derived from dried plant material like sorghum stover

entities.name: DRAFT AND DAIRY ANIMALS
entities.description: Animals that utilize sorghum stover as a source of dry fodder

entities.name: POULTRY FEED
entities.description: A use for sorghum mentioned in the title
